questions.text: Why is the story of the elephant army a powerful proof for the Quraysh?
questions.answer: Because it was close to their memory and showed Allah’s direct protection without their fighting.

questions.text: True/False: Surah Al-Fil teaches that armies always win if they have better weapons.
questions.answer: False. It shows Allah can destroy a powerful army by unexpected means.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about plots against sacred things?
questions.answer: Plots can appear strong, but Allah can nullify them completely.

questions.text: What is one spiritual lesson from the Kaaba being protected without human effort?
questions.answer: Reliance on Allah is real; Allah can defend His دين and sanctities in ways people cannot imagine.

questions.text: Why does Surah Al-Fil mention birds and stones rather than a human army?
questions.answer: To show Allah’s power: He can use small creatures to defeat great arrogance.

questions.text: What is one lesson about humility from Abraha’s failure?
questions.answer: Arrogance toward Allah’s sacred limits leads to humiliation, no matter how confident a person feels.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that sacred places are protected because people deserve it.
questions.answer: False. Protection is by Allah’s will and wisdom, not because people ‘earned’ it.

questions.text: How does this Surah strengthen belief in Allah’s control of events?
questions.answer: It shows Allah can turn a major historical plan into total ruin instantly.

questions.text: What is one practical way to apply the Surah’s lesson to personal life struggles?
questions.answer: Do your best, but trust Allah can change outcomes suddenly; don’t panic or despair.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Fil prepare the theme for Surah Quraysh?
questions.answer: It reminds of protection, then calls Quraysh to worship the Lord who gave them security and provision.

questions.text: What does being made like 'eaten straw' teach about worldly strength?
questions.answer: Worldly strength can become worthless; Allah can reduce it to nothing.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah encourages Muslims to rely on miracles and ignore planning.
questions.answer: False. It teaches reliance on Allah, not abandoning responsibility.

questions.text: What is the historical event this Surah refers to?
questions.answer: The Year of the Elephant ('Am al-Fil), when Abraha attempted to destroy the Kaaba.

questions.text: Who were the 'Companions of the Elephant'?
questions.answer: Abraha al-Ashram and his army from Yemen.

questions.text: Why did Abraha want to destroy the Kaaba?
questions.answer: To divert the pilgrims to his grand cathedral in Sana'a, Yemen.

questions.text: What did Allah do to their 'plot' (Kaydahum)?
questions.answer: He made it go astray / led it to ruin.

questions.text: What did Allah send against the army?
questions.answer: Birds in flocks (Tayran Ababil).

questions.text: What were the birds carrying?
questions.answer: Stones of hard-baked clay (Sijjiil).

questions.text: What was the result of the birds striking the army?
questions.answer: They were made like eaten straw ('Asfim-ma'kul).

questions.text: How many elephants were in Abraha's army?
questions.answer: Scholars say there was one large elephant (named Mahmud) or several.

questions.text: What happened to the elephant when it reached Makkah?
questions.answer: It knelt down and refused to move toward the Kaaba, despite being prodded.

questions.text: Who was the grandfather of the Prophet (SAW) who spoke to Abraha?
questions.answer: Abdul-Muttalib.

questions.text: What did Abdul-Muttalib say to Abraha regarding the Kaaba?
questions.answer: He said: 'I am the lord of the camels, but the House has a Lord who will protect it'.

questions.text: What is 'Asf' in this Surah?
questions.answer: Straw, leaves, or vegetation.

questions.text: Did the Quraysh have to fight the army of Abraha?
questions.answer: No, they fled to the mountains and watched as Allah destroyed the army.

questions.text: In what year did this event occur relative to the Prophet's birth?
questions.answer: It occurred in the same year Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was born.

questions.text: What is the lesson for the Quraysh in this Surah?
questions.answer: A reminder of Allah's favor upon them by protecting their city and the Kaaba.

questions.text: What is 'Sijjiil'?
questions.answer: Hardened clay or stones from Hellfire.

questions.text: How many verses are in Surah Al-Fil?
questions.answer: Five verses.

questions.text: What does 'Ababil' mean?
questions.answer: Groups, flocks, or flights of birds following one another.

questions.text: Does this Surah prove the sanctity of the Kaaba?
questions.answer: Yes, it shows that Allah Himself is the Protector of the House.
